title: inconsistent key-length validation idiom in aes vs cmac
description: AesKey::cache uses: if !matches!(key_bytes.len(), 16 | 24 | 32) { return Err(...) } CmacKey::cache uses: match key_bytes.len() { 16 | 24 | 32 => {} _ => return Err(...) } Both validate the same constraint (AES key size) on the same type (&[u8]). The idioms are semantically identical but visually inconsistent, making it look like a deliberate difference when it is not. Suggested rewrite: standardise on the if !matches!(...) form throughout, as it is more concise and reads as a guard clause.
